items.question: X has PMF p(0)=0.5, p(1)=0.5. What is H(X) in bits?
items.explanation: H(X) = −0.5 log₂ 0.5 − 0.5 log₂ 0.5 = 0.5 + 0.5 = 1 bit—the maximum for two outcomes.

items.question: X is deterministic with p(7)=1. What is H(X)?
items.explanation: No uncertainty: the sole outcome has probability 1, so −1 · log 1 = 0.

items.question: A fair six-sided die has H(X) equal to (in bits):
items.explanation: Uniform over six outcomes: H(X) = log₂ 6 ≈ 2.585 bits.

items.question: Bernoulli(p) entropy is maximized when p equals:
items.explanation: Binary entropy H(p) = −p log p − (1−p) log(1−p) peaks at p = 0.5 (1 bit).

items.question: Which change increases H(X) for a fixed number of support points?
options: Setting all but one p(x) to zero
items.explanation: Entropy is maximized by the uniform distribution over the same support; skewing toward one outcome lowers H(X).
